name: Muhsin ibn Hussain
title: Miscarried in Aleppo
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) unborn child who was miscarried in the city of Aleppo due to the pressures and cruelties of Yazid's army during captivity.
fullStory:
Muhsin ibn al-Hussain is one of the Master of Martyrs' (AS) children who never saw the world. His mother (likely Rabab or another of the Imam's wives) was pregnant during the event of Karbala.

After Ashura, this mourning mother, along with the other captives, while grieving the loss of her husband and loved ones, traveled the harsh route from Kufa to Sham on unsaddled camels.

The psychological pressure, hunger, thirst, and violence of the guards (like Zahr ibn Qays and Shimr) caused this child to be miscarried when the captives' caravan reached the vicinity of 'Aleppo' in modern-day Syria.

The body of this innocent unborn child was buried in Aleppo. Today, there is a shrine in Aleppo called 'Mashhad al-Siqt' (or Mashhad al-Muhsin), which is a place of pilgrimage for Shias and lovers of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS), serving as a document of the Karbala captives' oppression.
quoteArabic: وَسَقَطَ مِنْ نِسائِهِ حَمْلٌ فِي حَلَبٍ يُسَمّى مُحْسِناً...
quote: (Historical report:) And from the caravan's women, a fetus was miscarried in Aleppo, who was named Muhsin...
